# Solution

## (a) Implementation of `Canonicalize`

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, max(x.width, PAD))
}
```

This implementation preserves the kind and value (never changing what entity the id refers to), and sets the width to at least `PAD` (4 digits), keeping any existing width that is already 4 or more.

## (b) Specification Lemma

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); 
          c.kind == x.kind &&
          c.value == x.value &&
          c.width >= PAD &&
          c.width >= NumDigits(c.value) &&
          (x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width) &&
          Wellformed(c) &&
          (x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(c) == c)
{ }
```

**Explanation of each clause:**

1. **`c.kind == x.kind`** – Kind never changes
2. **`c.value == x.value`** – Numeric value never changes  
3. **`c.width >= PAD`** – Width is at least the minimum of 4 digits
4. **`c.width >= NumDigits(c.value)`** – The width can hold the value (wellformedness property)
5. **`(x.width >= PAD ==> c.width == x.width)`** – If already canonical, width unchanged (idempotence)
6. **`Wellformed(c)`** – Output is wellformed
7. **`(x.width >= PAD ==> Canonicalize(c) == c)`** – Canonicalizing a canonical id is a no-op

Together, these clauses capture that canonicalization preserves kind and value, left-zero-pads to a minimum of 4 digits, handles ids at or above that minimum unchanged, and is idempotent.
